unit.comment: /* * Created by Phil on 08/11/2010. * Copyright 2010 Two Blue Cubes Ltd. All rights reserved. * * Distributed under the Boost Software License, Version 1.0. (See accompanying * file LICENSE_1_0.txt or copy at http://www.boost.org/LICENSE_1_0.txt) */
// The "failing" tests all use the CHECK macro, which continues if the specific test fails.namespace ConditionTests

diff:delete.comment: // This allows us to see all results, even if an earlier check fails

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "Comparisons with int literals don't warn when mixing signed/ unsigned" )

diff:delete.comment: // Disable warnings about sign conversions for the next two tests
// - Currently only disabled for GCC/ LLVM. Should add VC++ too

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "Comparisons between unsigned ints and negative signed ints match c++ standard behaviour" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "Comparisons between ints where one side is computed" )

diff:delete.comment: // The problem with the ! operator is that it has right-to-left associativity.
// This means we can't isolate it when we decompose. The simple REQUIRE( !false ) form, therefore,
// cannot have the operand value extracted. The test will work correctly, and the situation
// is detected and a warning issued.
// An alternative form of the macros (CHECK_FALSE and REQUIRE_FALSE) can be used instead to capture
